title: Arculus duthieanus
taxonomy: Arculus duthieanus is classified within the family Annonaceae. This family comprises about 160 genera of flowering plants. The Annonaceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world, with many species found in Asia, Africa, and the Americas. Within the Annonaceae family, Arculus duthieanus belongs to the subfamily Annonoideae. The subfamily Annonoideae contains approximately 130 genera of flowering plants.

intro: Arculus duthieanus, also known as Himalayan Aractylis, is a plant species belonging to the Araliaceae family, commonly found in the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and analgesic properties, which help alleviate conditions like fever, pain, and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Arculus duthieanus include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used in the form of ointments or tinctures to harness its health benefits.
